text: Carbon-plated running shoes reduce running economy (oxygen cost per distance) by approximately 5.34 mL·kg⁻¹·km⁻¹ compared to non-plated shoes.
Carbon-plated footwear lowers metabolic demand during submaximal running by approximately 2-3% across multiple metabolic outcomes.
Carbon-plated shoes reduce metabolic cost by 0.38 W·kg⁻¹ compared to non-plated shoes during running.
Carbon-plated shoes reduce oxygen consumption during running by 1.23 mL·kg⁻¹·min⁻¹ compared to non-plated shoes.

The metabolic benefits of carbon-plated shoes cannot be attributed to the plate alone, as concurrent advanced footwear technology features such as compliant resilient foams likely contribute to the overall effect.
Advanced footwear technology combines compliant, resilient foams with a full-length carbon fiber plate that increases longitudinal bending stiffness to improve running metabolics.
